Class GameController.MonsterEntity

java.lang.Object
scenes.game.GameController.MonsterEntity
Enclosing class:
GameController
public static class GameController.MonsterEntity extends Object
Represents a live monster entity on the world map, including its position, AI state, and movement variables.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    boolean
    aggro
    true when the monster is chasing the player.
    double
    dx
    Current X component of the wander movement velocity.
    double
    dy
    Current Y component of the wander movement velocity.
    Monster
    monster
    The underlying monster logic object (stats, health, etc.).
    double
    moveTimer
    Timer (seconds) until the monster picks a new random wander direction.
    int
    type
    Difficulty tier: 0=easy, 1=medium, 2=hard.
    double
    x
    Current X pixel position on the world map.
    double
    y
    Current Y pixel position on the world map.

  • Field Details

    • monster

      public Monster monster
      The underlying monster logic object (stats, health, etc.).

    • x

      public double x
      Current X pixel position on the world map.

    • y

      public double y
      Current Y pixel position on the world map.

    • moveTimer

      public double moveTimer
      Timer (seconds) until the monster picks a new random wander direction.

    • dx

      public double dx
      Current X component of the wander movement velocity.

    • dy

      public double dy
      Current Y component of the wander movement velocity.

    • type

      public int type
      Difficulty tier: 0=easy, 1=medium, 2=hard.

    • aggro

      public boolean aggro
      true when the monster is chasing the player.

  • Constructor Details

    • MonsterEntity

      public MonsterEntity(Monster m, double x, double y, int type)
      Creates a new MonsterEntity.
      Parameters:
      m - the underlying monster logic object
      x - the initial x pixel position
      y - the initial y pixel position
      type - the difficulty tier (0=easy, 1=medium, 2=hard)
